title: Yol Areebi

{{Quote|Supplied an illegal mining facility in the Cron Drift with abducted natives of Endor as slave laborers.|Yol Areebi's bounty posting by the Republic Security Force|<ref name="SWBH" />}}
'''Yol Areebi''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] criminal who was alive during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Areebi had worked as a [[Slaver/Legends|enslaver]] and was wanted by the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] for taking kidnapped members of a native [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] to be used slave labor in an illegal [[Unidentified Cron Drift mining facility|mining facility]] in the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]]. During that year, Areebi worked at [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packing plant used as a front for selling [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]], which was located in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. There, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] claimed the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Areebi while Fett was [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrating]] the facility in search of information on [[Komari Vosa]], the leader of a secretive criminal organization known as the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]].

==Biography==
A [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dohlban]],<ref name="SWBH" /> the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Guineo]] criminal Yol Areebi lived during the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="SWBH" /> By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="date">The events of ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' take place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Areebi had worked as a [[Slaver/Legends|enslaver]], including an instance in which<ref name="SWBH" /> he<ref name="BHSG" /> took abducted members of a [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]] to an illegal [[Unidentified Cron Drift mining facility|mining facility]] in the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]] for use as slave labor. However, when he was in the [[Industrial District/Legends|Industrial District]] on the Republic's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Republic Security Force/Legends|Republic Security Force]] placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head for bringing slaves to the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facility. The bounty mentioned Areebi's last known location as the Industrial District and offered 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for capturing him alive and 1,000 credits for [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. At that [[Time/Legends|time]], Areebi was affiliated with a number of [[Slavery/Legends|enslavers]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|thugs-for-hire]].<ref name="SWBH" />

While he was in the Industrial Sector in 32 BBY, Areebi worked for [[Haugg Nerf Industries]], a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packing facility run by an individual named [[Groff Haugg]] as a front for the sale of [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]], a type of illegal [[Spice/Legends|narcotic]]. That year, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] [[Infiltration of Haugg Nerf Industries|infiltrated]] Haugg Nerf Industries in order to search for information that would lead him to [[Komari Vosa]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who was leading the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]], a secretive criminal cult rumored to be behind the increased demand for death sticks.<ref name="SWBH" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
After fighting through most of Haugg Nerf Industries in search of Haugg,<ref name="SWBH" /> Fett encountered Areebi in one of the facility's corridors.<ref name="BHSG" /> Areebi merely cowered as the bounty hunter approached and captured him to claim the bounty on his head.<ref name="SWBH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
As was common among members of the Guineo species, Yol Areebi stood over two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall with [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] skin of a [[Color/Legends|pale yellow]] color. A known associate of various enslavers and hired guns, Areebi was not above supplying an illegal facility with abducted individuals to be used as slave labor.<ref name="SWBH" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
However, Areebi did not act bravely when confronted by Jango Fett in Haugg Nerf Industries, where he cowered instead of fighting back.<ref name="SWBH" />
{{Endgame}}

==Equipment==
While working at Haugg Nerf Industries, Areebi wore a yellow [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] that had a gray plate on its front with two tubes running from it to the two silver-colored tanks attached to the back of his suit. The jumpsuit also had white stripes along the sleeves and around its collar. In addition to wearing the jumpsuit, Areebi wore light-gray [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], dark-gray [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and a pair of gray [[Goggles/Legends|goggles]].<ref name="SWBH" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:BountyHunter-Cover.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Yol Areebi first appeared in ''Star Wars: Bounty Hunter'']]
Yol Areebi first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', a [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[LucasArts]]<ref name="SWBH" /> and released on [[November 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="YBY">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> Before appearing in the game, he received an entry in ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', the game's corresponding guide, which was published by [[Prima Games]]<ref name="BHSG" /> on [[November 26]] of the same year.<ref name="Amazon">{{Amazon|url=Bounty-Hunter-Primas-Official-Strategy/dp/0761541640|text=Star Wars Bounty Hunter (Prima's Official Strategy Guide)}}</ref> In the game, Areebi is one of five bounties located in the Industrial District, which is the second level on Coruscant, the game's second world.<ref name="SWBH" />

In addition, Areebi is the only Guineo character in the game who will not fight; Areebi will merely cower if approached by the player. The game also does not describe the species of the Endor natives that he supplied to the illegal mining facility.<ref name="SWBH" /> Native species of the moon include [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]],<ref name="Dulok">{{SonyCite|url=players/guides.vm?id=80112|text=CorSec Database A-G|int=CorSec Database A-G}}</ref> [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]],<ref name="Ewok">{{CSWECite|I|244|Ewoks}}</ref> [[Gorph/Legends|Gorphs]],<ref name="Gorph">{{CSWECite|I|354|Gorph}}</ref> and [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzums]].<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> In ''Bounty Hunter'', the player controls Jango Fett, and since claiming Areebi's bounty is not essential to completing the game, the player has several different ways to go about doing so. The player can ignore Areebi entirely, kill him and not claim the bounty, kill him and claim the bounty, or capture him and claim the bounty. This article assumes that Fett captured Areebi alive, as doing so gives Fett the maximum reward possible.<ref name="SWBH" />
